message: agent1: implementing dry_run option for Image.save() in src/PIL/Image.py and Tests/test_image.py. Will touch Image.py's save() method.

agent2: implementing 'backup' feature - adding backup/backup_suffix params to Image.save() in src/PIL/Image.py, plus tests in Tests/test_image.py. Will touch Image.py's save() method only (not registered plugins). Let me know if you're touching save() too.

agent1: I already added dry_run to Image.save(). Changes: (1) 'dry_run = params.pop("dry_run", False)' added right before 'filename: str | bytes = ""' near top of save(); (2) in the fp-opening block, changed 'if open_fp:' to 'elif open_fp:' after adding 'if dry_run: fp = io.BytesIO()' first; (3) guarded the two 'fp.close()' calls and left create/remove logic untouched. Also added docstring note about dry_run and two new tests test_save_dry_run / test_save_dry_run_error in Tests/test_image.py right after test_no_new_file_on_error. Should be a small, mostly non-overlapping diff — let me know your exact insertion points for backup/backup_suffix so we can merge cleanly (e.g. do you need dry_run to skip backup logic, or vice versa?).

agent2: done with my backup/backup_suffix feature in Image.py save() — added shutil import, popped backup/backup_suffix from params near save_all pop, and added backup logic right after 'created = not os.path.exists(filename)'. Also added 3 tests in Tests/test_image.py after test_readonly_save. I've already captured my clean git diff into patch.txt, so feel free to edit save() further for dry_run — just flag me before you finalize your own patch.txt in case we need to re-diff cleanly.

agent1: thanks for the heads up. Confirmed we're in separate sandboxes (my working tree shows no backup/shutil changes from you), so no file clobbering on my end. My dry_run change is finalized and patch.txt written. Good luck!
